name: Faylen's Bagels
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine yeast and 1/2 cup warm water in a measuring cup, stirring with a fork until dissolved.
2. In a bowl, combine 2 cups flour, salt, and sugar. Add the dissolved yeast and remaining 1/2 cup of warm water. Beat vigorously with a whisk or spoon for 2 minutes.
3. Gradually add enough of the remaining flour (about 1/4 cup at a time) to make a soft dough. Turn out onto a floured surface.
4. Knead until smooth and elastic, adding as little flour as necessary, about 8-10 minutes. Don't skimp on the kneading!
5. Place the dough in a bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise until doubled in size (45-60 minutes). Watch carefully.
6. Punch down the dough and divide into 12 equal portions (about 3-4 oz each). Weigh the dough on a kitchen scale for accuracy and even size.
7. Form the portions into smooth balls, gently pulling the sides of the dough down and tucking the ends underneath. Place on a floured surface and let rest 10 minutes.
8. Using a finger or wooden spoon handle, punch a hole in the center of each piece and twirl or stretch into a bagel shape.
9. Cover with a towel and let rest another 20-30 minutes.
10. Bring a large shallow pan of water to a boil (6 cups), adding 1 tablespoon sugar and 1 tablespoon salt. Gently drop 3 or 4 bagels at a time into the simmering water.
11. Poach each side for 2 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and place on a towel to cool.
12. Brush each bagel with an egg white wash (1 egg white mixed with 1 tablespoon water). Optionally sprinkle with salt, poppy seeds, minced garlic or onion, sesame seeds, etc.
13. Place the bagels on a greased baking sheet and bake at 375°F for 30 minutes. For a crisper crust, bake for 10 minutes, then brush with egg wash and sprinkle with desired topping, returning to the oven for an additional 20 minutes.
14. Remove from the baking sheet and cool on wire racks.
These freeze well.
